chat.body: referencing eee-335-final-lecture walkthrough and the eee335 final lecture review pdf in the raw folder can you explain how we get the Rin and Ro equations for the comman gate amplifier acting as a buffer

Before I dive in — **stop.** This is a teacher session, not an oracle session. My job is to make you derive these formulas yourself. You'll remember the CG resistance derivations 3× longer if you fight through them than if I hand them to you.

Let me check the raw final-review PDF you mentioned and the existing wiki pages on CG so I can calibrate.

Hey Jayden — welcome back.

You asked a moment ago about deriving $R_{\text{in}}$ and $R_o$ for the common-gate amplifier as a buffer, and I was about to push you through it Socratically (rather than just handing you the algebra). Want to pick that up?

If yes, your first task — **before I show you anything from the slides or the walkthrough** — is to attempt this:

> Sketch the CG small-signal circuit on paper. Just the basics: input at the source, gate AC-grounded, output at the drain, with $r_o$ included. Apply a test voltage $v_x$ at the **source** to find $R_{\text{in}}$. Tell me: what current flows out of $v_x$ into the source terminal? Even a guess with hand-waving is fine — I want to see your reasoning.

Take a shot. We'll fix whatever's wrong together. (And if you'd rather do something else entirely — quiz, walkthrough a different topic, just chat — say the word.)
here where I am right now I have vx = ix+gmvgs(ro) + RL(ix), for your previous question ix is the current flowing into the source terminal
here where I am right now I have vx = ix+gmvgs(ro) + RL(ix), for your previous question ix is the current flowing into the source terminal
